text.heading: Cancer Patient Seeks N1.3 for Treatment
text.p: A 35-year old breast cancer patient at Federal Medical Centre (FMC) Abakaliki, Mrs. Caroline Chukwu Odi, has made a passionate appeal to public spirited individuals, organisations and the government to come to her aid in raising N1.3 million required for the treatment of her ailment.
Speaking in an interview with THISDAY on her hospital bed, Odi said she was at the verge of suffering paralysis which might result to her death and pleaded for financial assistance to save her life.

Also corroborating his wife‚Äôs appeal, Mr. George Chukwu Odi, a petty trader at Abuja stated that he has expended all his life‚Äôs savings in trying to provide medical solution to her wife‚Äôs sickness but the situation remains life threatening.

Explaining how the problem started, Odi, who hails from Isu Echara in Onicha Local Government Area of Ebonyi State, said it started in 2007 when his wife complained that she was having pains on her left breast and when she was taken to Gwagwalada Specialist Hospital, it was confirmed that she had cancer of the breast.

According to him, his wife was taken to Asokoro hospital where it was further discovered that the cancer was about to spread to the right breast also. The medical experts however told them that both breasts can still be treated as the problem hasn‚Äôt degenerated badly as shown by tests conducted.

He said that she was subsequently given minor treatment and discharged, only for the ailment to come back some months after with more severity resulting her being admitted at National Hospital Abuja where she was given full treatment in addition to removing the left breast through surgical operation.

‚ÄúAfter that we felt it was ok, as we were only going for medical check ups. She returned to the village and went to Aba to stay briefly with her brother. It was there the problem came back and became worse than before. We now came to Ebonyi State University Teaching Hospital (EBSUTH), but after we stayed for two weeks, they went on strike and we were then transferred to FMC,‚Ä? he narrated.

Odi said that he was forced to relocate to Ebonyi abandoning his means of livelihood in order to be with his wife, adding that presently he has no money to continue with the treatment or to take care of his wife and their only 12 year old son.

In a reaction, the consultant physician handling Odi‚Äôs case at the FMC, Dr. O.O Okidi, told newsmen that the patient was being managed by the hospital for advanced cancer of the breast, adding however that her condition has gone beyond baseline treatment for breast cancer, but that she can only benefit from second line therapy which he said was very expensive.
Okidi put the cost of the six-course second line treatment to N1,390,440 in order to save Odi from dying, insisting that the case was treatable.
